Document:
# Rule 8: Character Names Use a unique, roleplay-appropriate character name consisting of a first and last name. You may add short meaningful nicknames in quotation marks (for example, Derek "Double D" Donahue, Jane "Crusher" Sanchez). It is forbidden to use the name and surname of another character, intentionally copy or imitate them, or create names that are similar in spelling or pronunciation with the intent of impersonating another character. You are allowed to be a relative (for example, a brother or sister) of another player’s character. The administrator reserves the right to make the final decision on whether a name is acceptable.
- Names containing ranks, titles, honorifics, offensive language, celebrity names, or pop culture references are not allowed. - Roman numerals are allowed, but in reasonable quantity. - Nicknames must not be written entirely in UPPERCASE. - A nickname may consist of at most two words. - Names and nicknames must not be memes, contain insults, sexual innuendo, or any adult content. - Xenomorph prefixes must also comply with these rules.
## Examples of acceptable names - Arthur Walker - Jeremy Bennett - Becky Jones
## Examples of unacceptable names and callsigns - "Tugoserik" - Adolf Hitler - Donald Trump - "Nazi" - Mike Rotch - David Weyland - Ellen Ripley - Any puns, sexual references, or references to prohibited themes.
## Exceptions A name composed of common names (for example, John Williams) is allowed as long as the player is not clearly attempting to imitate or reference a famous person.
